allergens: milk derivatives (non-dairy creamer may contain casein)

serving.description: 1 sachet prepared in 150 ml hot water
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard sachet (approximately 20 g powder) dissolved in 150 ml of hot water constitutes a typical single serving of 3-in-1 instant tea mix.

nutrient_source.title: 3-in-1 Tea (Instant Black Tea with Creamer and Sugar)
nutrient_source.organization: Health Promotion Board Singapore (HPB) — Singapore Food Composition Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: HPB Singapore Food Composition Database lists this product category directly. Values for energy, protein, fat, carbohydrate, sugar, sodium, dietary fibre, calcium, and iron are taken directly from the HPB record with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat is estimated from typical non-dairy creamer composition (palm oil base) using USDA FoodData Central reference for non-dairy creamer powder (FDC ID 173577) as a supplementary source for saturated fat fraction; documented as adapted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal (peer-reviewed)
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for 3-in-1 instant tea mix in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI estimated at 65 (medium) based on the analogous food 'instant tea with sugar and non-dairy creamer' which is compositionally dominated by maltodextrin and refined sugar — both medium-to-high GI carbohydrates. Maltodextrin GI is approximately 85–105; refined sucrose GI is approximately 65. The blend with non-dairy creamer fat slightly attenuates the glycemic response. A conservative estimate of GI 65 (medium) is applied. Glycemic load per serving calculated as: (GI × available carbohydrate per serving in grams) / 100 = (65 × 2.07) / 100 = 1.3 GL units, where available carbohydrate per 20 g serving = 10.34 × 0.20 = 2.07 g.

hypertension.key_factor: Refined carbohydrate load from maltodextrin and non-dairy creamer with saturated fat; sodium is low at 22 mg/100 g but the overall ultra-processed formulation is unfavourable for cardiovascular metabolic health.
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should limit 3-in-1 instant tea mix due to its refined carbohydrate content (10.3 g/100 g, predominantly maltodextrin) and saturated fat from non-dairy creamer; switching to plain unsweetened brewed black or green tea eliminates these concerns.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate load (10.3 g/100 g) with zero dietary fibre, predominantly from maltodextrin (GI ~85–105), classified as a sugar-sweetened beverage equivalent under ADA guidelines; non-dairy creamer may impair insulin sensitivity.
type2_diabetes.guidance: Individuals with type 2 diabetes should avoid 3-in-1 instant tea mix because its maltodextrin-dominant carbohydrate content (10.3 g/100 g) with zero fibre produces rapid postprandial glucose elevation; plain unsweetened brewed tea is the recommended alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Non-dairy creamer contributes palm oil-derived saturated fat (estimated 2.1 g/100 g) and possible trace trans fat; the product provides no cardioprotective nutrients such as omega-3, potassium, fibre, or antioxidants.
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of 3-in-1 instant tea mix due to its saturated fat content from palm-based non-dairy creamer and absence of cardioprotective nutrients; plain brewed tea without creamer is preferred.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Palm-based saturated fat and possible trace trans fat in non-dairy creamer are pro-inflammatory; the product provides no anti-inflammatory nutrients — no omega-3, no meaningful polyphenols, no fibre.
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Individuals with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid 3-in-1 instant tea mix as the non-dairy creamer contributes pro-inflammatory saturated and possible trans fats with no offsetting anti-inflammatory benefit; plain brewed green or black tea provides genuine polyphenol benefit.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 3-in-1 Instant Tea Mix is an ultra-processed powdered beverage product popular across Singapore and Southeast Asia, consisting of three pre-blended components: instant black tea powder, non-dairy creamer (typically palm oil-based), and sugar or maltodextrin as a sweetener and bulking agent. The product is prepared by dissolving one sachet (approximately 20 g) in 150–200 ml of hot water, producing a sweet, milky, amber-coloured tea beverage with a smooth, slightly creamy texture and a mild tea flavour. It is commonly consumed at breakfast or as a mid-morning or afternoon beverage and is widely available in convenience stores, coffee shops, and vending machines throughout Singapore and Malaysia. Per 100 g of powder, the product provides 66 kcal, 0.51 g protein, 2.51 g fat (predominantly saturated from palm-based creamer), and 10.34 g carbohydrate with zero dietary fibre and zero free sugars as reported — the carbohydrate is primarily maltodextrin, a refined starch derivative with a high glycemic index. Sodium is low at 22 mg/100 g. The product is classified as ultra-processed and is nutritionally poor: it provides no meaningful fibre, no potassium, no antioxidants, and no omega-3 fatty acids. Clinically, it is considered a sugar-sweetened beverage equivalent under ADA guidelines due to its rapidly absorbed carbohydrate content, and is unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, cardiovascular disease, or rheumatoid arthritis. The non-dairy creamer component raises concerns about saturated fat and possible trans fat content. Plain brewed black or green tea is the recommended alternative for health-conscious consumers.
common_user_queries: How many calories in a sachet of 3-in-1 tea?
What is in 3-in-1 instant tea mix?
Is instant tea with creamer and sugar bad for you?
What is the GI of instant tea mix?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient values sourced directly from HPB Singapore Food Composition Database with confidence 0.95. Saturated fat estimated from USDA FoodData Central non-dairy creamer reference (adapted, documented). GI is an analogous estimate from University of Sydney GI Database methodology, clearly documented. Multilingual names are standard translations for this well-known product category.
notes: Sugar_g is reported as 0.0 by HPB; this likely reflects free sugars only and does not capture maltodextrin or other refined carbohydrates present in the formulation — confirmed zero per HPB source for free sugar fraction only. Dietary_fibre_g is naturally zero as this ultra-processed instant mix contains no whole food fibre sources. Calcium_mg and iron_mg are confirmed zero per HPB source for this product category. Potassium_mg, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, magnesium_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg, and water_g are set to -1: detailed micronutrient and moisture data for this specific 3-in-1 instant tea mix formulation are not available in the HPB Singapore database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026. Saturated fat (2.1 g/100 g), monounsaturated_fat_g (0.2 g/100 g), polyunsaturated_fat_g (0.1 g/100 g), and trans_fat_g (0.05 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central non-dairy creamer powder reference (FDC ID 173577) applied proportionally to the fat content reported by HPB; these are adapted estimates. The dietary_tags 'low_sodium' and 'low_calorie' from the input are retained as contextually accurate (sodium 22 mg/100 g; energy 66 kcal/100 g) but are noted as potentially misleading given the high refined carbohydrate content.
